Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiff S Request For Entry Of Court Judgment By Court
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was set for Prove- Up for a Request for Default Judgment against Defendant Daniella Rand.

Dept:505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K.Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:54:40 a.m., pausing at 9:57:14 a.m. resuming at 10:02:29 a.m. pausing at 10:06:52 a.m. resuming at 10:08:29 a.m. and concluding at 10:17:46 a.m.(D505)

Meha Raja, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff John C. Atwater and Plaintiff Diana Nelson via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Daniella Rand not appearing.
Witness Carol Snell sworn to testify for the Plaintiff via Zoom Video Conference.

Counsel to provide a summary and offers proof as to the request for judgment to the court.
Counsel to provide the date bankruptcy case against defendant was dismissed.
Counsel examines witness.
The elements for a request for judgment is discussed.
Court examines witness. Witness confirms that declarations and statements made are true and correct.

Good cause appearing, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has proven its prima facie case and rules in favor of the Plaintiff against the Defendant as follows:

Damages $150,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest at 10% $57,375.60
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $0
Total Judgment $207,375.60

Proposed judgment as amended is signed and executed this day.

Proposed judgment as amended signed and executed this day.

Notice Of Hearing On Request For Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for request for judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:05:20 a.m. and concluding at 10:25:42 a.m.

Court Reporter: Janet Wood; CSR 463; 949-636-8719; janetwoodcsr@gmail.com

Plaintiff John C. Atwater appearing represented by Olivia Bona, Esq.
Defendant Daniella Rand not appearing.

JOHN C. ATWATER and CAROL SNELL are sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.
Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

JOHN C. ATWATER is examined by the Court.
CAROL SNELL is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
The Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to prove breach. The landlord failed to return it's deposit within 21 days and the interest required. Court finds in favor of Plaintiff and awards the following judgment:

1. Damages: $151,100.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $6,260.27
3. Costs: $2,000.00

Attorney fees waived.

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $159,360.27

Proposed judgment signed and filed.
